questions.text: If two quantities x and y vary directly with each other, then:
questions.explanation: In direct proportion, the ratio of the two quantities (x/y) is always a constant (k).

questions.text: If x and y vary inversely with each other, then:
questions.explanation: In inverse proportion, the product of the two quantities (x * y) remains constant (k).

questions.text: If 15 workers can build a wall in 48 hours, how many workers will be required to do the same work in 30 hours?
questions.explanation: This is inverse proportion. 15 * 48 = x * 30 => x = (15 * 48) / 30 = 24.

questions.text: A car travels 432 km on 48 liters of petrol. How far will it travel on 20 liters of petrol?
If x and y are in direct proportion and x = 5 when y = 20, what is y when x = 8?
A machine in a soft drink factory fills 840 bottles in six hours. How many bottles will it fill in five hours?
Which of the following is an example of inverse proportion?
options: Number of articles and their cost
Speed and time taken for a fixed journey
Area of land and its cost
questions.explanation: If speed increases, time taken decreases, which is inverse proportion.

questions.text: If the cost of 7 kg of sugar is ₹147, what is the cost of 12 kg of sugar?
If x and y are in inverse proportion and x = 10 when y = 6, find y when x = 15.
A map is drawn to a scale of 1 cm = 500 km. If two cities are 4 cm apart on the map, the actual distance is:
questions.explanation: Scale is direct proportion: 1/500 = 4/x => x = 2000 km.

questions.text: If 6 pipes are required to fill a tank in 1 hour 20 minutes, how long will it take if only 5 pipes of the same type are used?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: (6 * 80) = (5 * x) => 480/5 = 96 minutes = 1 hr 36 min.

questions.text: If the weight of 12 sheets of thick paper is 40 grams, how many sheets of the same paper would weigh 2500 grams?
Which of these is NOT in direct proportion?
options: Workers and time to finish a job
Side of a square and its perimeter
questions.explanation: Workers and time are in inverse proportion; more workers mean less time.

questions.text: If 52 men can finish a piece of work in 35 days, in how many days will 28 men finish it?
A photograph of a bacteria enlarged 50,000 times attains a length of 5 cm. What is the actual length?
If x = 2 and y = 10 are in direct proportion, what is the constant k (x/y)?
A car takes 2 hours to reach a destination by travelling at 60 km/h. How long will it take at 80 km/h?
In a camp, there is food for 100 people for 20 days. If 25 more people join, how long will the food last?
If x and y are in direct proportion, as x increases:
questions.explanation: In direct proportion, both quantities increase or decrease together.

questions.text: If x and y are in inverse proportion, as x increases:
questions.explanation: In inverse proportion, if one quantity increases, the other decreases in the same ratio.

questions.text: A school has 8 periods a day each of 45 minutes duration. If the school has 9 periods, how long would each period be?
If 20 apples cost ₹100, what is the cost of 3 apples?
If x/y = k, then x and y are in:
questions.explanation: x/y = k is the mathematical definition of direct proportion.

questions.text: If xy = k, then x and y are in:
questions.explanation: xy = k is the mathematical definition of inverse proportion.

questions.text: If 10 meters of cloth cost ₹250, then 15 meters will cost:
An electric pole, 14 metres high, casts a shadow of 10 metres. What is the height of a tree that casts a shadow of 15 metres?
Which of these pairs are in inverse proportion?
options: Number of workers and time taken to complete work
questions.explanation: More workers take less time, which is inverse variation.

questions.text: If a car covers 120 km in 2 hours, how much distance will it cover in 5 hours at same speed?
If x1/y1 = x2/y2, then x and y are in ________ variation.
questions.explanation: This represents the equality of ratios in direct variation.

questions.text: If x1 * y1 = x2 * y2, then x and y are in ________ variation.
questions.explanation: This represents the constant product in inverse variation.

questions.text: If 8 men can finish a task in 40 days, 16 men will finish it in:
If the length of a shadow is proportional to the height of the object, what kind of proportion is it?
questions.explanation: Taller objects cast longer shadows at a given time; thus, it is direct proportion.

questions.text: If 24 workers can build a wall in 15 days, how many workers can build it in 10 days?
The price of 1 kg of apples is ₹120. The price of 500 g is:
If 2 pens cost ₹10, find the cost of 10 pens.
If the area of a circle increases with its radius, this variation is:
questions.explanation: They increase together, so it is direct variation (though not linear).

questions.text: If 7 machines take 2 days to finish a task, how many days will 1 machine take?
If x varies directly as y and x=3 when y=9, what is k?
If x varies inversely as y and x=3 when y=9, what is k?
If 12 men can reap a field in 8 days, in how many days can 16 men reap it?
